repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1 concerns proxy-null bridge fibers, not a full-law mediator-defier response polytope.

published_prior_art.cite: tian_pearl_2000_probabilities_causation gives generic sharp PN bounds; Dawid–Murtas–Musio (2014) uses mediator information under no mediator-outcome confounding.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal gives sharp PN intervals by deleting mediator-defier response types under arbitrary mediator-outcome response dependence.

published_prior_art.cite: murtas_dawid_musio_2017_new_bounds_pc_mediation and dawid_humphreys_musio_2024_bound_causes_effects_mediators study mediator-informed PC bounds under stronger process information.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1 addresses proxy-null fibers and pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 ordinal principal-stratum compatibility; neither contains a PN mediator-defier-face strictness frontier.

published_prior_art.cite: No verified source supplies an observable iff characterization of strict contraction from deleting mediator-defier types while retaining arbitrary mediator-outcome response dependence; the missed 2026 partial-diagram paper is close but imposes mediator conditional-independence constraints and does not analyze this frontier.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal supplies tie-aware inference for the paired sign-free/mediator-monotone PN endpoint map.

published_prior_art.cite: fang_santos_2019_directionally_differentiable supplies generic directional-delta/bootstrap theory, while zhang_wang_miao_2025_causal_attribution_confidence supplies close PN-bound inference.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question oeq:canonical-defier-threshold
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits the close 2026 paper “Bounding Probabilities of Causation with Partial Causal Diagrams,” which explicitly gives mediator-informed PN/PNS optimization programs; distinguish its conditional-independence constraints and lack of a monotonicity strictness frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question oeq:canonical-defier-threshold
soundness_flags.one_line: With T defined only as a threshold derived from the same dual pieces defining G, the claimed strict-containment iff T(p)>0 can be obtained by defining T as a transform of G_L and G_U; specify an independent primitive observable characterization of T and prove its equivalence.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem thm:pn-endpoint-limit
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The T-coordinate limit and bootstrap claim assumes that oeq:canonical-defier-threshold first yields a finite directionally differentiable observable representation of T.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Conditional on that independently derived finite representation, the endpoint-and-threshold plug-in map has the stated directional-delta and modified-bootstrap route.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the circular threshold handle with a primitive support-slack or basis-feasibility functional stated directly in p, then prove that it equals the endpoint-gain criterion rather than defining it from that criterion.

Add “Bounding Probabilities of Causation with Partial Causal Diagrams” to related work and give a statement-level non-representability comparison: its mediator program enforces conditional-independence constraints absent from the arbitrary M-Y-confounded response-type class.

Include a nondegenerate binary worked law p, its active bases and primal endpoint-attaining couplings, and a direct calculation showing both gains strictly positive on an explicit relatively open neighborhood.

recommended_next_step: Revise the threshold into an independently characterized observable object and repair the close published-work comparison, then re-review the field-grade partial-identification kernel.
